$comment: D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF (C23 6.7.2.5): `typeof`/`typeof_unqual` resolve to an operand's type. A RUNTIME wrap-at-256 store+load through a `typeof(base)`-declared unsigned char (200+argc+59 = 260 -> u8 -> 4) is the empirical proof that typeof(expr) flows the operand's underlying type into codegen (store/load at u8 width; widen via U8 ZExt). exit = wrapped(4) + sizeof(typeof(unsigned short))(2) + sizeof(typeof(base))(1) + cast-to-typeof(31) + 4 = 42; an int-typed typeof(base) would give a different exit. argc is a runtime arg so the wrap + cast are not const-folded even under release. Data-model-independent (unsigned char/short widths hold under LP64 and LLP64), so the one exit code holds on all four targets incl. the shipped release pipeline. Also exercises the typeof TYPE-NAME form (sizeof(typeof(unsigned short))), the typeof EXPRESSION form (typeof(base)/sizeof), typeof_unqual(volatile int) (a plain-int compute; the strip is pinned by the semantic tests), and a cast whose type-name is a typeof.
